title: auto-update doesn't work with docker-compose / API
body:
### Issue Description

When I run `podman auto-update --dry-run` on containers created through `docker-compose` via podman system service, it fails, quoting "raw-image name is empty".

Looks like `rawImageName` is only set in [CLI](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/375eb045cad3475a7ef7b7a610195ca2ac46694f/cmd/podman/containers/create.go#L169) and [play](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/375eb045cad3475a7ef7b7a610195ca2ac46694f/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L856) but not in [API handler](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/375eb045cad3475a7ef7b7a610195ca2ac46694f/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_create.go#L118).


### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a `docker-compose.yaml` file:

    ```yaml
    version: '3'
    services:
      foo:
        labels:
          io.containers.autoupdate: registry
          PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT: foo
        image: docker.io/library/nginx
    ```
2. `DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/podman/podman.sock docker-compose up -d`
3. `podman auto-update --dry-run`


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: locally auto-updating container "37e4bd663b555b85cba38b8a91126ca5caaee5f134d029bd6185de3389755611": raw-image name is empty
```

### Describe the results you expected

`podman auto-update` should work as if the container is created through the CLI.
```
UNIT        CONTAINER                              IMAGE                    POLICY      UPDATED
podman      2e6524593a8b (affectionate_goldstine)  docker.io/library/nginx  registry    false
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @SEIAROTg.

Auto updates do not work via the API.  The [docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-auto-update.1.html) mention the requirement to run Podman in systemd: _"Note that podman auto-update relies on systemd. The systemd units are expected to be generated with [podman-generate-systemd --new](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.html#--new), or similar units that create new containers in order to run the updated images. Systemd units that start and stop a container cannot run a new image."_
I will update the docs a bit to highlight this requirement more.

Thanks for the reply @vrothberg.

I don't see how the systemd requirement conflicts with the API though. Systemd units can always be created afterwards through `podman-generate-systemd` or in my case, through [Arion](https://docs.hercules-ci.com/arion/). The situation should be similar to `podman create` or `podman run`, which work with auto-update. Did I miss anything?

Technically the distinction is that we didn't set `rawImageName` in the API code path. Maybe we should set that anyway?

> I don't see how the systemd requirement conflicts with the API though. Systemd units can always be created afterwards through `podman-generate-systemd` or in my case, through [Arion](https://docs.hercules-ci.com/arion/). The situation should be similar to `podman create` or `podman run`, which work with auto-update. Did I miss anything?

For auto update to work, the units must be created via `podman generate systemd --new` and `--new` requires the units to be created via Podman on the CLI.  Remote and API don't work.  That is mentioned in the docs over [here](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.html#new).

The workflow with `podman generate systemd` is far from optimal as it requires a lot of manual steps, and to redo all of them after update.  Hence, we deprecated it in favor of [Quadlet](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html).  Since you are using Compose, you may enjoy using Quadlet as well.
